req.title: Inter-facility referral (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for Clinician Referral System
Requirement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility;
             the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.
Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain enums & exceptions (would normally live in production source code)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ReferralStatus(Enum):
    DRAFT = "DRAFT"
    PENDING_CONSENT = "PENDING_CONSENT"
    CONSENT_GRANTED = "CONSENT_GRANTED"
    CONSENT_DENIED = "CONSENT_DENIED"
    SENT = "SENT"
    CANCELLED = "CANCELLED"
    COMPLETED = "COMPLETED"


class PermissionDeniedError(Exception):
    """Raised when the requesting user lacks required permissions."""


class ConsentDeniedError(Exception):
    """Raised when the patient has not consented to the referral."""


class MissingMandatoryFieldError(Exception):
    """Raised when mandatory referral fields are absent or empty."""


class InvalidStateTransitionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a referral is moved to an incompatible state."""


class SecureMessageError(Exception):
    """Raised when secure message delivery fails."""


class DuplicateReferralError(Exception):
    """Raised when an identical active referral already exists."""


class FacilityUnavailableError(Exception):
    """Raised when the target facility cannot accept referrals."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures – lightweight fakes / stubs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_user():
    return {
        "user_id": "clinician-001",
        "name": "Dr. Alice Smith",
        "role": "CLINICIAN",
        "npi": "1234567890",
        "facility_id": "facility-src-001",
        "permissions": ["send_referral", "view_patient_record"],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def admin_user():
    return {
        "user_id": "admin-001",
        "name": "Bob Jones",
        "role": "ADMIN",
        "facility_id": "facility-src-001",
        "permissions": ["view_referral"],          # intentionally NO send_referral
    }


@pytest.fixture
def nurse_user():
    return {
        "user_id": "nurse-001",
        "name": "Carol White",
        "role": "NURSE",
        "facility_id": "facility-src-001",
        "permissions": [],                         # no permissions at all
    }


@pytest.fixture
def patient():
    return {
        "patient_id": "patient-999",
        "name": "John Doe",
        "dob": "1980-05-15",
        "mrn": "MRN-12345",
        "consent_on_file": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def patient_no_consent(patient):
    p = dict(patient)
    p["consent_on_file"] = False
    return p


@pytest.fixture
def target_facility():
    return {
        "facility_id": "facility-dst-007",
        "name": "Metro Cardiology Center",
        "active": True,
        "accepts_referrals": True,
        "secure_message_endpoint": "https://metro-cardio.example.com/secure-msg",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def unavailable_facility(target_facility):
    f = dict(target_facility)
    f["accepts_referrals"] = False
    return f


@pytest.fixture
def valid_referral_payload(patient, target_facility, clinician_user):
    return {
        "patient_id": patient["patient_id"],
        "referring_clinician_id": clinician_user["user_id"],
        "source_facility_id": clinician_user["facility_id"],
        "target_facility_id": target_facility["facility_id"],
        "reason_for_referral": "Chest pain evaluation",
        "urgency": "ROUTINE",
        "clinical_notes": "Patient complains of intermittent chest pain.",
        "icd10_codes": ["R07.9"],
        "created_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_referral_service():
    """Returns a MagicMock that emulates the ReferralService interface."""
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.send_referral.return_value = {
        "referral_id": "ref-abc-123",
        "status": ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT.value,
    }
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def mock_message_gateway():
    """Emulates the secure-messaging gateway."""
    gw = MagicMock()
    gw.send_secure_message.return_value = {"message_id": "msg-xyz-456", "delivered": True}
    return gw


@pytest.fixture
def mock_consent_service():
    """Emulates the consent-check service."""
    cs = MagicMock()
    cs.check_consent.return_value = True
    return cs


@pytest.fixture
def mock_permission_service():
    """Emulates the RBAC / permission-check service."""
    ps = MagicMock()
    ps.has_permission.return_value = True
    return ps


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – thin orchestrator that wires the mocks together.
# Production code would import the real class; here we define the contract.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ReferralOrchestrator:
    """
    Thin orchestrator used in tests to express the system contract.
    In production this would be the real application service.
    """

    def __init__(self, referral_service, message_gateway,
                 consent_service, permission_service):
        self.referral_service = referral_service
        self.message_gateway = message_gateway
        self.consent_service = consent_service
        self.permission_service = permission_service

    def send_referral(self, requesting_user: dict, payload: dict,
                      target_facility: dict) -> dict:
        # 1. Permission check
        if not self.permission_service.has_permission(
            requesting_user["user_id"], "send_referral"
        ):
            raise PermissionDeniedError(
                f"User {requesting_user['user_id']} lacks 'send_referral' permission."
            )

        # 2. Mandatory field validation
        mandatory = [
            "patient_id", "referring_clinician_id", "source_facility_id",
            "target_facility_id", "reason_for_referral", "urgency",
        ]
        missing = [f for f in mandatory if not payload.get(f)]
        if missing:
            raise MissingMandatoryFieldError(
                f"Missing mandatory fields: {missing}"
            )

        # 3. Facility availability
        if not target_facility.get("accepts_referrals"):
            raise FacilityUnavailableError(
                f"Facility {target_facility['facility_id']} is not accepting referrals."
            )

        # 4. Consent check
        if not self.consent_service.check_consent(payload["patient_id"]):
            raise ConsentDeniedError(
                f"Patient {payload['patient_id']} has not granted consent."
            )

        # 5. Persist referral
        result = self.referral_service.send_referral(payload)

        # 6. Send secure message to target facility
        self.message_gateway.send_secure_message(
            endpoint=target_facility["secure_message_endpoint"],
            referral_id=result["referral_id"],
            patient_id=payload["patient_id"],
        )

        return result


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ✅  HAPPY-PATH TESTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestReferralHappyPath:

    def test_successful_referral_sends_secure_message(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """A valid referral should persist and trigger exactly one secure message."""
        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        result = orchestrator.send_referral(
            clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
        )

        assert result["referral_id"] == "ref-abc-123"
        assert result["status"] == ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT.value

        mock_message_gateway.send_secure_message.assert_called_once_with(
            endpoint=target_facility["secure_message_endpoint"],
            referral_id="ref-abc-123",
            patient_id=valid_referral_payload["patient_id"],
        )

    def test_successful_referral_triggers_consent_check(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """A consent check must be performed for every referral attempt."""
        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        orchestrator.send_referral(
            clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
        )

        mock_consent_service.check_consent.assert_called_once_with(
            valid_referral_payload["patient_id"]
        )

    def test_permission_check_precedes_business_logic(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Permission must be evaluated before any downstream service is called."""
        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        orchestrator.send_referral(
            clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
        )

        mock_permission_service.has_permission.assert_called_once_with(
            clinician_user["user_id"], "send_referral"
        )

    def test_referral_returns_pending_consent_status_on_creation(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Newly created referrals must start in PENDING_CONSENT status."""
        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        result = orchestrator.send_referral(
            clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
        )

        assert result["status"] == ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT.value


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ❌  PERMISSION-DENIAL TESTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestPermissionDenial:

    def test_admin_user_cannot_send_referral(
        self,
        admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service,
    ):
        """Admin without 'send_referral' permission must be rejected."""
        permission_service = MagicMock()
        permission_service.has_permission.return_value = False  # admin denied

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError) as exc_info:
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        assert admin_user["user_id"] in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_nurse_user_cannot_send_referral(
        self,
        nurse_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service,
    ):
        """Nurse with no permissions must be rejected."""
        permission_service = MagicMock()
        permission_service.has_permission.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                nurse_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

    def test_no_secure_message_sent_on_permission_denial(
        self,
        admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service,
    ):
        """When permission is denied no secure message must be dispatched."""
        permission_service = MagicMock()
        permission_service.has_permission.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        mock_message_gateway.send_secure_message.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_consent_check_performed_on_permission_denial(
        self,
        admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service,
    ):
        """Consent check must NOT be invoked before permissions are validated."""
        permission_service = MagicMock()
        permission_service.has_permission.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        mock_consent_service.check_consent.assert_not_called()

    def test_referral_service_not_called_on_permission_denial(
        self,
        admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service,
    ):
        """Persistence layer must never be reached when permission is denied."""
        permission_service = MagicMock()
        permission_service.has_permission.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                admin_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        mock_referral_service.send_referral.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ❌  MISSING MANDATORY ELEMENT TESTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestMissingMandatoryFields:

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = [
        "patient_id",
        "referring_clinician_id",
        "source_facility_id",
        "target_facility_id",
        "reason_for_referral",
        "urgency",
    ]

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_single_mandatory_field_raises(
        self,
        missing_field,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Each individually absent mandatory field must raise MissingMandatoryFieldError."""
        payload = dict(valid_referral_payload)
        del payload[missing_field]

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryFieldError) as exc_info:
            orchestrator.send_referral(clinician_user, payload, target_facility)

        assert missing_field in str(exc_info.value)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_empty_string_mandatory_field_raises(
        self,
        missing_field,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Empty-string values for mandatory fields are equivalent to absent."""
        payload = dict(valid_referral_payload)
        payload[missing_field] = ""

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryFieldError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(clinician_user, payload, target_facility)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_none_valued_mandatory_field_raises(
        self,
        missing_field,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """None values for mandatory fields must also raise."""
        payload = dict(valid_referral_payload)
        payload[missing_field] = None

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryFieldError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(clinician_user, payload, target_facility)

    def test_multiple_missing_mandatory_fields_raises_with_all_names(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """When multiple fields are missing the error should list them all."""
        payload = dict(valid_referral_payload)
        del payload["patient_id"]
        del payload["urgency"]

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryFieldError) as exc_info:
            orchestrator.send_referral(clinician_user, payload, target_facility)

        error_msg = str(exc_info.value)
        assert "patient_id" in error_msg
        assert "urgency" in error_msg

    def test_secure_message_not_sent_on_missing_fields(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Secure message must not be sent when validation fails."""
        payload = dict(valid_referral_payload)
        del payload["reason_for_referral"]

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryFieldError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(clinician_user, payload, target_facility)

        mock_message_gateway.send_secure_message.assert_not_called()

    def test_empty_payload_raises_missing_mandatory_fields(
        self,
        clinician_user, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """A completely empty payload must raise MissingMandatoryFieldError."""
        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryFieldError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(clinician_user, {}, target_facility)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ❌  CONSENT-DENIAL TESTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestConsentDenial:

    def test_referral_blocked_when_patient_has_no_consent(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Referral must be blocked when consent check returns False."""
        consent_service = MagicMock()
        consent_service.check_consent.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(ConsentDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

    def test_secure_message_not_sent_without_consent(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """A secure message must never leave the system if consent is absent."""
        consent_service = MagicMock()
        consent_service.check_consent.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(ConsentDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        mock_message_gateway.send_secure_message.assert_not_called()

    def test_referral_not_persisted_without_consent(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Referral must not be stored in the persistence layer when consent fails."""
        consent_service = MagicMock()
        consent_service.check_consent.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(ConsentDeniedError):
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        mock_referral_service.send_referral.assert_not_called()

    def test_consent_denial_error_contains_patient_id(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """ConsentDeniedError must identify which patient was blocked."""
        consent_service = MagicMock()
        consent_service.check_consent.return_value = False

        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(ConsentDeniedError) as exc_info:
            orchestrator.send_referral(
                clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
            )

        assert valid_referral_payload["patient_id"] in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_consent_check_receives_correct_patient_id(
        self,
        clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility,
        mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
        mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
    ):
        """Consent service must be called with the exact patient identifier."""
        orchestrator = ReferralOrchestrator(
            mock_referral_service, mock_message_gateway,
            mock_consent_service, mock_permission_service,
        )

        orchestrator.send_referral(
            clinician_user, valid_referral_payload, target_facility
        )

        mock_consent_service.check_consent.assert_called_once_with(
            valid_referral_payload["patient_id"]
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ❌  CONFLICTING STATE TRANSITION TESTS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestConflictingStateTransitions:
    """
    These tests validate that the referral service itself enforces
    legal state-machine transitions and rejects illegal ones.
    """

    # Allowed transitions (illustrative subset):
    #   DRAFT -> PENDING_CONSENT
    #   PENDING_CONSENT -> CONSENT_GRANTED | CONSENT_DENIED
    #   CONSENT_GRANTED -> SENT
    #   SENT -> COMPLETED | CANCELLED
    #   CANCELLED -> (terminal – no further transitions)
    #   COMPLETED -> (terminal – no further transitions)

    @pytest.fixture
    def state_service(self):
        """
        Minimal fake that tracks referral state and enforces transitions.
        In production this would be the real ReferralStateService.
        """

        LEGAL_TRANSITIONS = {
            ReferralStatus.DRAFT: {ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT, ReferralStatus.CANCELLED},
            ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT: {ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED, ReferralStatus.CONSENT_DENIED, ReferralStatus.CANCELLED},
            ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED: {ReferralStatus.SENT, ReferralStatus.CANCELLED},
            ReferralStatus.CONSENT_DENIED: set(),          # terminal
            ReferralStatus.SENT: {ReferralStatus.COMPLETED, ReferralStatus.CANCELLED},
            ReferralStatus.CANCELLED: set(),               # terminal
            ReferralStatus.COMPLETED: set(),               # terminal
        }

        class _StateService:
            def __init__(self):
                self._store = {}

            def create(self, referral_id: str) -> ReferralStatus:
                self._store[referral_id] = ReferralStatus.DRAFT
                return ReferralStatus.DRAFT

            def transition(self, referral_id: str, new_status: ReferralStatus) -> ReferralStatus:
                current = self._store.get(referral_id)
                if current is None:
                    raise KeyError(f"Unknown referral: {referral_id}")
                if new_status not in LEGAL_TRANSITIONS[current]:
                    raise InvalidStateTransitionError(
                        f"Cannot move referral {referral_id} "
                        f"from {current.value} to {new_status.value}."
                    )
                self._store[referral_id] = new_status
                return new_status

            def current_status(self, referral_id: str) -> ReferralStatus:
                return self._store[referral_id]

        return _StateService()

    # -- Legal-transition smoke tests ----------------------------------------

    def test_draft_to_pending_consent_is_legal(self, state_service):
        state_service.create("ref-1")
        result = state_service.transition("ref-1", ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT)
        assert result == ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT

    def test_pending_consent_to_consent_granted_is_legal(self, state_service):
        state_service.create("ref-2")
        state_service.transition("ref-2", ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT)
        result = state_service.transition("ref-2", ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED)
        assert result == ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED

    def test_consent_granted_to_sent_is_legal(self, state_service):
        state_service.create("ref-3")
        state_service.transition("ref-3", ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT)
        state_service.transition("ref-3", ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED)
        result = state_service.transition("ref-3", ReferralStatus.SENT)
        assert result == ReferralStatus.SENT

    def test_sent_to_completed_is_legal(self, state_service):
        state_service.create("ref-4")
        state_service.transition("ref-4", ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT)
        state_service.transition("ref-4", ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED)
        state_service.transition("ref-4", ReferralStatus.SENT)
        result = state_service.transition("ref-4", ReferralStatus.COMPLETED)
        assert result == ReferralStatus.COMPLETED

    # -- Illegal-transition tests ---------------------------------------------

    def test_draft_to_sent_raises_invalid_transition(self, state_service):
        """Cannot skip PENDING_CONSENT and jump straight to SENT."""
        state_service.create("ref-bad-1")
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            state_service.transition("ref-bad-1", ReferralStatus.SENT)

    def test_draft_to_completed_raises_invalid_transition(self, state_service):
        """Cannot jump from DRAFT directly to COMPLETED."""
        state_service.create("ref-bad-2")
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            state_service.transition("ref-bad-2", ReferralStatus.COMPLETED)

    def test_pending_consent_to_sent_raises_invalid_transition(self, state_service):
        """SENT requires passing through CONSENT_GRANTED first."""
        state_service.create("ref-bad-3")
        state_service.transition("ref-bad-3", ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            state_service.transition("ref-bad-3", ReferralStatus.SENT)

    def test_cancelled_to_any_state_raises_invalid_transition(self, state_service):
        """CANCELLED is a terminal state – no further transitions allowed."""
        state_service.create("ref-bad-4")
        state_service.transition("ref-bad-4", ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT)
        state_service.transition("ref-bad-4", ReferralStatus.CANCELLED)

        for target_status in [
            ReferralStatus.DRAFT,
            ReferralStatus.PENDING_CONSENT,
            ReferralStatus.CONSENT_GRANTED,
            ReferralStatus.SENT,
            ReferralStatus.

"""
Pytest test suite for Clinician Referral System
Requirement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility;
             the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.
Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain enums & exceptions (would normally live in production source code)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ReferralStatus(Enum):
    DRAFT = "DRAFT"
    PENDING_CONSENT = "PENDING_CONSENT"
    CONSENT_GRANTED = "CONSENT_GRANTED"
    CONSENT_DENIED = "CONSENT_DENIED"
    SENT = "SENT"
    CANCELLED = "CANCELLED"
    COMPLETED = "COMPLETED"


class PermissionDeniedError(Exception):
    """Raised when the requesting user lacks required permissions."""


class ConsentDeniedError(Exception):
    """Raised when the patient has not consented to the referral."""


class MissingMandatoryFieldError(Exception):
    """Raised when mandatory referral fields are absent or empty."""


class InvalidStateTransitionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a referral is moved to an incompatible state."""


class SecureMessageError(Exception):
    """Raised when secure message delivery fails."""


class DuplicateReferralError(Exception):
    """Raised when an identical active referral already exists."""


class FacilityUnavailableError(Exception):
    """Raised when the target facility cannot accept referrals."""

# Fixtures – lightweight fakes / stubs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def clinician_user():
    return {
